subject: Product Management
title: Superhuman's PMF Engine
description: A quick win: understand Superhuman's PMF Engine.

hook.text: Rahul Vohra, CEO of Superhuman, turned the 40% test into a systematic engine for improving product-market fit.

buildup.text: He surveyed users, segmented by 'very disappointed' responses, and asked those users what they loved most.

discovery.text: Then he asked 'somewhat disappointed' users what would make the product better — and built exactly that.

twist.text: By focusing only on what 'very disappointed' users loved and 'somewhat disappointed' users needed, he had a precise roadmap.

climax.text: Superhuman's PMF score rose from 22% to 58%. Vohra proved that PMF can be engineered, not just discovered by luck.

punchline.text: PMF isn't luck. It can be measured and engineered.

quiz.question: How did Superhuman improve its product-market fit?
options: Segmenting survey responses to build what 'somewhat disappointed' users needed
